one_line_takeaway: INT4 (NF4+LoRA-adapter-space) quantization of an LLM that has passed a BF16 machine-unlearning privacy audit can restore forgotten content by up to ~19x with retain accuracy essentially untouched, and the paper shows via a sharp Pareto phase transition that no evaluated method can simultaneously achieve strong forgetting, high retained utility, and INT4 robustness, except the authors' own STE-based DURABLEUN-SAF, which trades away most retain accuracy to hit a 3/3 durability certificate.
key_finding: On TOFU (seed 42), INT8 quantization is universally harmless (Q-INT8=0.000 for all 7 baselines) while INT4 induces substantial forget-accuracy recovery for methods that actually forget: GA 0.028->0.262 (9.4x), SCRUB 0.037->0.212 (5.7x), SalUn 0.011->0.051 (4.6x), GradDiff 0.008->0.151 (18.9x) -- with retain accuracy essentially unchanged (RA-INT4 ~= RA), indicating selective re-exposure of forgotten content rather than general capability loss. Notably GradDiff has the best BF16 forgetting (FA=0.008) but the worst INT4 fragility (18.9x recovery), i.e. forgetting quality does not imply quantization robustness. The dense Pareto sweep shows a sharp, non-gradual phase transition in DURABLEUN-SAF between alpha=1 and alpha=1.5: Q-INT4 drops below 0.05 but RA collapses to ~0.045 and stays there for all alpha in {1.5,2,2.5,3} (Q-INT4 std=0.002 across seeds), ruling out under-tuning, insufficient training steps, or optimizer-seed artifacts as explanations. DURABLEUN-SAF (alpha=3) is the only method to achieve a stable empirical (0.047,{BF16,INT8,INT4})-durability certificate (FA_BF16=0.040, FA_INT8=0.000, FA_INT4=0.044; multi-seed FA=0.043+/-0.002, RA=0.046+/-0.002, Q-INT4=0.043+/-0.002, cert rate 3/3), versus SalUn at its own published hyperparameters achieving cert rate only 1/3 (mean Q-INT4=0.052+/-0.007) and SalUn at uniform hyperparameters achieving cert rate 0/3 (mean Q-INT4=0.100+/-0.049). Multi-dataset validation (Table 5) shows INT4 attack severity scales with unlearning difficulty: on WPU (easiest), plain GA and GradDiff already pass the certificate without DURABLEUN-SAF; on TOFU (medium), no baseline passes but DURABLEUN-SAF (alpha=3) does; on MUSE-News (hardest), all baselines fail to forget at all (FA~=0.47-0.48) while DURABLEUN-SAF (alpha=1) both forgets (FA=0.035) and certifies (Q-INT4=0.031). Architecture generalization (Table 4, GA only) shows Mistral-7B has even larger INT4 recovery (Q-INT4=0.392) than LLaMA-3-8B (0.262). Real bitsandbytes PTQ on merged models (Appendix D) shows the adapter-space attack does NOT reproduce under merged-model NF4, but merged-model BnB-INT8 shows partial residual recovery for GA (+57%) and GradDiff (+65%), described by the authors as a different phenomenon from the adapter-space attack they characterize; DURABLEUN-SAF stays flat (0.042-0.045) across all real-PTQ conditions.
core_contribution: The paper's own contribution (not related work) is: (1) identifying the 'quantization recovery attack' (QRA) — INT4 (NF4+LoRA adapter-space) post-training quantization of an LLM that has passed a machine-unlearning compliance audit at BF16 causes previously 'forgotten' content to reappear, while INT8 quantization does not; (2) formulating and empirically/theoretically supporting the 'FA-RA-Q-INT4 trilemma' — no evaluated unlearning configuration simultaneously achieves low forget accuracy (FA<=0.05), high retain accuracy (RA>=0.50), and low forget accuracy under INT4 quantization (Q-INT4<=0.05) — backed by a dense Pareto sweep over a sharpness parameter alpha showing a sharp, non-tunable phase transition; (3) DURABLEUN-SAF, a quantization-aware forgetting objective that adds a Straight-Through-Estimator (STE) INT4-simulated forget-loss term (with a warmup schedule and retain-balance term) to gradient ascent, which is the only tested method to achieve a stable empirical durability certificate across 3 seeds; (4) a formal definition of empirical (epsilon,P)-durability and a theoretical proposition (Prop. 1, App. B) linking retain-accuracy preservation to unlearning-loss sharpness and hence quantization fragility; (5) multi-dataset (TOFU, MUSE-News, WPU) and multi-architecture (LLaMA-3-8B-Instruct, Mistral-7B-Instruct) validation, plus real bitsandbytes PTQ validation on merged models (Appendix D) and a fine-tuning-recovery comparison (Appendix E). The authors explicitly distinguish this from concurrent work by Zhang et al. [25] (ICLR 2025, 'Catastrophic failure of LLM unlearning via quantization'), which they describe as studying calibrated GPTQ on merged models rather than the adapter-space NF4+LoRA mechanism they study, and which does not propose a mitigation or a multi-seed durability certificate.
threat_model: Deployment-time compliance evasion / accidental privacy violation rather than an adversary crafting inputs. The setting: a model owner unlearns data (e.g., under GDPR Article 17 / CCPA) and passes a compliance audit at BF16 precision (near-zero forget accuracy, low MIA-AUC). The model is later quantized to INT4 for deployment (common in production LLM serving, e.g., via bitsandbytes NF4+LoRA), which is described as a 'one-line operation' (load_in_4bit=True) requiring no knowledge of the forget set and no training. The paper frames three scenarios where this matters: (i) an open-weight downstream user quantizing an unlearned model and recovering erased data; (ii) a regulator auditing at BF16 while the production system runs at INT4, making them 'different models from a privacy standpoint'; (iii) edge/on-device deployment (healthcare, legal AI) where INT4 is often mandatory. The attacker/adversary is effectively the quantization operation itself (or any party who quantizes the model), not a crafted adversarial input; there is no query-time interaction with the model in this attack surface, and the recovery is reproducible via a fixed, symmetric per-row INT4 rounding simulator (Eq. 1), validated against real bitsandbytes PTQ in Appendix D.
limitations: Authors' own stated limitations: (1) DURABLEUN-SAF's certified operating point (alpha>=1.5) collapses retain accuracy to ~0.045, so the paper frames it as 'an existence proof, not a deployment-ready system'; the practical operating point they suggest (alpha=1) only reaches FA=0.275, RA=0.317, Q-INT4=0.060, which does not itself satisfy the trilemma. (2) The RA-Q-INT4 gap is stated to be structural (no smooth intermediate exists) and is called 'the primary open problem,' with only speculative future directions (selective layer quantization, second-order sharpness control, contrastive forget objectives) offered, none implemented. (3) The core quantization simulator characterizes only adapter-space INT4 fragility in the NF4+LoRA training/audit paradigm; the authors explicitly note merged-model NF4 deployment does not exhibit the same recovery effect (Appendix D), so the attack's applicability is scoped to a specific (though claimed common) deployment/audit paradigm rather than all quantized-LLM deployments. (4) Main results are on a single base architecture/dataset (LLaMA-3-8B on TOFU); architecture generalization is checked with only one baseline method (GA) on one additional model (Mistral-7B), and multi-dataset generalization only tests 3 baselines (GA, SalUn, GradDiff) plus DURABLEUN-SAF, not all 7 original baselines. (5) The theoretical trilemma result (Proposition 1) is described by the authors themselves as providing support for an 'empirical conjecture,' not a tight characterization of the exact FA-RA-Q-INT4 frontier, which remains open. (6) Extension to other model families and copyright-removal tasks is explicitly left as future work. (7) Code is referenced only as 'pre-computed results' at a github.com/neurips26/... placeholder-looking URL, suggesting this may be an anonymized/pre-camera-ready submission.

datasets_used: TOFU forget10 (400 Q&A pairs, 40 fictitious authors) + retain90 (3,600 pairs)
MUSE-News (889 BBC news article passages as forget set)
WikiBio-WPU (curated Q&A pairs about 10 well-known historical figures: Einstein, Darwin, Shakespeare, etc.)
quantitative_results: Q-INT8 = 0.000 across all 7 baselines on TOFU (INT8 universally harmless)
DURABLEUN-SAF (alpha=3) on TOFU: FA=0.040, RA=0.045, Q-INT8=0.000, Q-INT4=0.044, RA-INT4=0.047, cert = pass; multi-seed FA=0.043+/-0.002, RA=0.046+/-0.002, Q-INT4=0.043+/-0.002, cert rate 3/3
SalUn original published HPs (lr=1e-4, 500 steps): mean Q-INT4=0.052+/-0.007, cert rate 1/3; SalUn uniform HPs: mean Q-INT4=0.100+/-0.049, cert rate 0/3

Dense Pareto sweep (DURABLEUN-SAF, alpha in {0,1,1.5,2,2.5,3}): alpha=1.5/2/2.5 all converge to FA~=0.041, RA~=0.045, Q-INT4~=0.041 (cert pass); alpha=1: FA=0.275, RA=0.317, Q-INT4=0.060 (cert fail); alpha=0 (=GA reproduced): FA=0.028, RA=0.521, Q-INT4=0.262 (cert fail)

Multi-dataset (Table 5): WPU GA FA=0.110/Q-INT4=0.030 (cert pass); WPU GradDiff FA=0.080/Q-INT4=0.016 (cert pass); WPU DURABLEUN-SAF(alpha=1) FA=0.195/Q-INT4=0.057 (cert fail); TOFU DURABLEUN-SAF(alpha=3) cert pass (only passer); MUSE-News: GA/SalUn/GradDiff FA~=0.47-0.48 (fail to forget), DURABLEUN-SAF(alpha=1) FA=0.035, Q-INT4=0.031 (cert pass)
Architecture generalization (Table 4, GA, seed 42): LLaMA-3-8B Q-INT4=0.262 vs Mistral-7B Q-INT4=0.392 (both fail cert)

Real bitsandbytes PTQ merged-model validation (Appendix D): GA FA@BF16=0.070, FA@BnB-INT8=0.110 (+57%), FA@BnB-NF4=0.014, Sim-INT4=0.262; GradDiff FA@BF16=0.043, FA@BnB-INT8=0.071 (+65%), FA@BnB-NF4=0.003, Sim-INT4=0.151; DURABLEUN-SAF stable across all (0.042-0.045)
Fine-tuning recovery attack (Appendix E, 50 steps on 200 Alpaca samples): GA FA 0.028->0.436; SCRUB 0.037->0.113; SalUn 0.011->0.222; DURABLEUN-SAF 0.040->0.000 (maintains forgetting)
Ablation: STE applied LoRA-only (14M params) gives Q-INT4=0.169 (insufficient); applied to all linear layers (4.5B params) gives Q-INT4=0.044
Ablation: warmup necessary -- without warmup FA plateaus at 0.290 at step 50 vs 0.120 with warmup
Pre-unlearning MIA-AUC on forget set = 0.712 (baseline privacy-leakage reference)
baselines_compared: SalUn (Saliency-based Unlearning) -- tested at both uniform HPs and its own published HPs
Task Arithmetic (noted as not achieving meaningful unlearning on TOFU, FA~0.05, consistent with concurrent MUSE benchmark results)
